segments.text: Now Moses was tending the flock of Jethro his father-in-law, the priest of Midian, and he led the flock to the far side of the wilderness and came to Horeb, the mountain of God.
There the angel of the Lord appeared to him in flames of fire from within a bush. Moses saw that though the bush was on fire it did not burn up.
Moses thought,
I will go over and see this strange sight—why the bush does not burn up.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: When the Lord saw that he had gone over to look, God called to him from within the bush,
Moses! Moses!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And Moses said,
Here I am.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Do not come any closer. Take off your sandals, for the place where you are standing is holy ground.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then he said,
I am the God of your father, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: At this, Moses hid his face, because he was afraid to look at God.
I have indeed seen the misery of my people in Egypt. I have heard them crying out because of their slave drivers, and I am concerned about their suffering.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: So I have come down to rescue them from the hand of the Egyptians and to bring them up out of that land into a good and spacious land, a land flowing with milk and honey—the home of the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Perizzites, Hivites and Jebusites.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And now the cry of the Israelites has reached me, and I have seen the way the Egyptians are oppressing them.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: So now, go. I am sending you to Pharaoh to bring my people the Israelites out of Egypt.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But Moses said to God,
Who am I that I should go to Pharaoh and bring the Israelites out of Egypt?
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: I will be with you. And this will be the sign to you that it is I who have sent you: When you have brought the people out of Egypt, you will worship God on this mountain.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Moses said to God,
Suppose I go to the Israelites and say to them, 'The God of your fathers has sent me to you,' and they ask me, 'What is his name?' Then what shall I tell them?
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: I am who I am. This is what you are to say to the Israelites: 'I am has sent me to you.'
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Say to the Israelites, 'The Lord, the God of your fathers—the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob—has sent me to you.' This is my name forever, the name you shall call me from generation to generation.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Go, assemble the elders of Israel and say to them, 'The Lord, the God of your fathers—the God of Abraham, Isaac and Jacob—appeared to me and said: I have watched over you and have seen what has been done to you in Egypt.'
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And I have promised to bring you up out of your misery in Egypt into the land of the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Perizzites, Hivites and Jebusites—a land flowing with milk and honey.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The elders of Israel will listen to you. Then you and the elders are to go to the king of Egypt and say to him, 'The Lord, the God of the Hebrews, has met with us. Let us take a three-day journey into the wilderness to offer sacrifices to the Lord our God.'
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But I know that the king of Egypt will not let you go unless a mighty hand compels him.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So I will stretch out my hand and strike the Egyptians with all the wonders that I will perform among them. After that, he will let you go.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And I will make the Egyptians favorably disposed toward this people, so that when you leave you will not go empty-handed.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Every woman is to ask her neighbor and any woman living in her house for articles of silver and gold and for clothing, which you will put on your sons and daughters. And so you will plunder the Egyptians.
segments.speaker: God
